caption: User Management
description: User Management events report lifecycle management of a user and account management tasks that are performed, such as a password change or reset, a user being created, updated, deleted, disabled, enabled, locked out or unlocked. There are two ways updates to the state of a user may be expressed: Using the Update activity, for example when updating multiple items at one time, the updated_user reflects the changes made to user. Using discrete activities, such as Assign Privileges the state change is expressed by the privileges attribute. Optionally, on successful changes, the updated_user reflects the new state of the user.
name: user_management

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the activity that triggered the event. The target of each activity is the user attribute.

1.caption: Create
1.description: Create a user. Populate the user object with details of the user.

2.caption: Update
2.description: Update the user. The updated user details should be populated in the updated_user attribute.

7.caption: Unlock
7.description: Unlock a previously locked out user.

8.caption: Password Change
8.description: An attempt to change the user's password.

9.caption: Password Reset
9.description: An attempt to reset the user's password.

10.caption: Attach Policies
10.description: Attach one or more IAM Policies to the user. Use policies as policies to be attached.

11.caption: Detach Policies
11.description: Detach one or more IAM Policies from the user. Use policies as policies to be detached.

12.caption: Enable MFA Factors
12.description: Enable one or more authentication factors for the user. Use auth_factors as factors to be enabled.

13.caption: Disable MFA Factors
13.description: Disable one or more authentication factors for the user. Use auth_factors as factors to be disabled.

14.caption: Assign Privileges
14.description: Assign privileges to the user. Use privileges as privileges to be assigned.

15.caption: Remove Privileges
15.description: Remove privileges from the user. Use privileges as privileges to be removed.

16.caption: Assign Roles
16.description: Assign one or more roles to the user. Use iam_roles as roles to be assigned.

17.caption: Remove Roles
17.description: Remove one or more roles from the user. Use iam_roles as roles to be removed.

18.caption: Add Programmatic Credentials
18.description: Add credentials to a role. Use programmatic_credentials as credentials to be added.

19.caption: Remove Programmatic Credentials
19.description: Remove credentials from a role. Use programmatic_credentials as credentials to be removed.

auth_factors.description: Details about the authentication factors associated with the MFA Factor Enable/Disable activities.

iam_roles.description: One or more roles assigned to or removed from a user.

policies.description: Details about the IAM policies associated with the Attach/Detach Policy activities.

privileges.description: Set of privileges assigned to a user.

resources.description: Resources that the privileges, policies, and roles give access to.

user.description: The user that was a target of an activity.

updated_user.description: The intended state of the user after the update. On Success, represents the actual post-update state.
